text: Artificial intelligence brings great benefits, but also raises important ethical questions. One key issue is bias. If an AI is trained on data that reflects historical prejudices, it may make unfair decisions in areas like hiring or loan applications. This directly relates to fairness, as such systems can disadvantage certain groups. Another concern is privacy; many AI applications collect vast amounts of personal data, so it is essential to handle this information carefully. To build trust, developers must also prioritise transparency, explaining clearly how algorithms work and why they reach particular conclusions. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires balancing innovation with these ethical principles, ensuring that technology serves everyone fairly.
